visual_base.visual_narrative_log: (1) A young Asian woman with long dark hair, wearing a brown off-the-shoulder top, stands in a brightly lit warehouse-style supermarket aisle. She holds a large purple-and-white plastic bag of Sanritsu Heike Pie biscuits with both hands, smiling at the camera. Behind her are rows of shelving stacked high with white boxes and more bags of the same product. A large Costco Wholesale logo is overlaid at the top. (2) The camera remains at eye-level as the woman gestures toward the shelves behind her, indicating the popularity of the product. She points with her right hand while holding the bag with her left. The background shows various household goods in typical Costco bulk packaging. Yellow and white text subtitles appear at the bottom of the frame, along with a [Variety] label. (3) The setting shifts to a home living room with warm lighting. The woman, now wearing a grey bucket hat, sits at a glass table cluttered with opened biscuit packaging. She picks up an individually wrapped biscuit, opens it, and breaks it in half to show the flaky layers and raisin filling. Close-up shots highlight the texture of the biscuit and its packaging details while she eats.
visual_base.text_narrative_log: (1) [Logo]: Costco Wholesale. [Subtitle]: 不用去日本 在好市多也可以買到的. (2) [Variety]: 三立葡萄派家庭包. (3) [Subtitle]: 一大包不用300元呢. (4) [Subtitle]: 哇 而且它銷量好誇張 你看 剩下這些了. (5) [Subtitle]: 剛上就剩兩箱 手速要快 回去試試看好不好吃. (6) [Variety]: 殿堂級的點心. [Subtitle]: 聽說它是 殿堂級的點心. (7) [Subtitle]: 我最喜歡吃葡萄派了 回去試試看 非常厲害的. (8) [Variety]: 葡萄千層酥. [Subtitle]: 葡萄千層酥 它上面的葡萄是用洋酒釀的. (9) [Subtitle]: 有一點白蘭地的香氣 然後它單個單個包裝 真的非常典雅. (10) [Subtitle]: 我們打開以後啊 可以看到 滿滿的葡萄乾之外. (11) [Variety]: 非常蓬鬆. [Subtitle]: 它的酥皮也是非常蓬鬆 而且是千層的. (12) [Subtitle]: 一層一層 超級無敵好吃. (13) [Variety]: 一口接一口. [Subtitle]: 然後它上面裹的糖啊 是那種很像砂糖樹的糖 你以為很甜 可是實際上吃下去 比例會非常的剛好 然後葡萄的香氣這會一口接一口. (14) [Subtitle]: 整個的甜度就是非常完美 非常適合大人食用. (15) [Subtitle]: 我覺得它是像伴手禮 那種精緻點心 而不是隨便的餅乾. (16) [Subtitle]: 所以它一個8塊 我覺得是值得買的.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 female main character appears throughout the video.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A young woman with long dark hair, wearing a brown off-the-shoulder ruffled top, and later a grey bucket hat.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: The initial scene is a Costco warehouse aisle with high shelves stacked with bulk goods. The second scene is a residential living room with a pink sofa, a glass coffee table, and soft indoor lighting.

audio_base.audio_narrative_log: (1) The video opens with a female voice speaking in a high-arousal, energetic tone with clear articulation. An upbeat, mid-tempo electronic pop BGM starts immediately, featuring a strong rhythmic beat and bright synth textures. There is a faint background hum suggesting a large public space, though the vocal signal is dominant and clear. (2) A sudden crinkling sound of a plastic bag being handled triggers this phase. The narrator continues speaking with high pitch variability, emphasizing the price and availability of the snacks. The BGM maintains a consistent volume, providing a lively bed for the commentary. (3) The acoustic texture shifts slightly as the narrator's voice becomes more descriptive. A distinct, high-frequency 'crunch' sound effect is heard as she presumably bites into something, followed by the soft sound of plastic rustling and a digital 'pop' sound effect used for emphasis during the review.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: Linear: Discovery -> Purchase/Price -> Detailed Tasting -> Final Recommendation.
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video aims to recommend and review 'Sanritsu Heike Pie' biscuits found at Costco. It combines an exciting 'find' at the supermarket with a detailed sensory review at home, highlighting the product's quality and value for money to entice viewers to purchase it.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The creator specifically showcases a branded product from a specific retailer (Costco) and provides positive feedback on taste and price.

high_inference.freeDescribe: The video effectively uses on-screen text and variety-style graphics to emphasize key selling points like 'temple-level snack' and 'not too sweet,' which aligns with common marketing tropes in Taiwanese food vlogs.
